assertions.assertion: Defines prior with correct support for all 4 parameters (beta>0, gamma>0, 0<i0<1, 0<p<1)
assertions.evidence: Lines 54-59: beta ~ Gamma(2, 0.3) (positive), gamma ~ Gamma(2, 0.15) (positive), i0 ~ Beta(2, 50) (0,1), p ~ Beta(5, 2) (0,1).

assertions.assertion: Uses bf.Adapter() with explicit constraint handling
assertions.evidence: Lines 118-132: adapter = bf.Adapter() with .constrain() calls for all 4 parameters.

assertions.assertion: Calls .constrain() with lower=0 for beta and gamma
Calls .constrain() with lower=0, upper=1 for i0 and p
assertions.evidence: Line 125: .constrain("i0", lower=0, upper=1) and Line 126: .constrain("p", lower=0, upper=1).

assertions.assertion: Routes the daily case counts through summary_variables (not inference_conditions)
assertions.evidence: Line 132: .concatenate(["daily_cases"], into="summary_variables"). No inference_conditions routing present.

assertions.assertion: Uses a summary network appropriate for sequential data (TimeSeriesTransformer, TimeSeriesNetwork, or SetTransformer if treating days as exchangeable is justified and documented)
assertions.evidence: Lines 97-105: bf.networks.TimeSeriesTransformer(...) used. Analysis notes justify: 'Daily case counts over 30 days are an ordered sequence — day order matters for epidemic dynamics.'

assertions.assertion: Starts with the Small model configuration from references/model-sizes.md
assertions.evidence: Lines 94-109: Comments state 'Small config'. TimeSeriesTransformer uses embed_dims=(32,32), num_heads=(2,2), mlp_depths=(1,1), mlp_widths=(64,64). Analysis notes confirm: 'Started with Small config per the skill's hard rule.'

assertions.assertion: Sets summary_dim to approximately 2x the number of parameters (around 8)
assertions.evidence: Line 94 comment: '4 parameters -> summary_dim = 2 * 4 = 8'. Line 98: summary_dim=8.

assertions.assertion: Passes validation_data= to the fit_* call
Runs in-silico diagnostics before any real-data inference
assertions.evidence: Lines 196-226: In-silico diagnostics run via workflow.simulate(300), workflow.plot_default_diagnostics(), workflow.compute_default_diagnostics(), and check_diagnostics(). Real-data inference comes after.

assertions.assertion: Checks all 4 parameters individually against house thresholds
assertions.evidence: Lines 208-215: check_diagnostics(metrics) called on per-parameter diagnostics DataFrame. Lines 218-222 enforce STOP/WARN decisions. Analysis notes confirm: 'Hard stop if any parameter exceeds ECE > 0.10 or NRMSE > 0.15'.

assertions.assertion: Does NOT flatten the time series into inference_conditions
assertions.evidence: Lines 118-132: adapter routes daily_cases via .as_time_series(["daily_cases"]) then .concatenate(["daily_cases"], into="summary_variables"). No inference_conditions key used.
